text:
Pure PLG companies are now trading at LOWER multiples than sales-led peers.

First time since 2020.

Read that again, because the entire "PLG is the future" narrative is built on assumptions that stopped being true about 18 months ago.

The data nobody's quoting:

ICONIQ's 2024 benchmarks show hybrid GTM motions (PLG + sales-assist) deliver 1.4x higher NRR than pure PLG once you cross $10M ARR.

Gong's 2024 numbers are even more brutal: enterprise deals sourced from PLG signups have 23% lower ACV but require nearly identical sales cycles to outbound deals.

You're doing the same work for less money.

Here's what's actually happening: every company we held up as a PLG poster child quietly bolted on sales motions after $50M ARR. Amplitude. Pendo. ChiliPiper. The playbook everyone copied wasn't the playbook that got them to $200M.

The reframe that's helped my team:

PLG is an acquisition channel. Not a GTM strategy.

Treat it like paid search or content. It feeds the top of funnel. It is not the funnel.

If you're a PM at a mid-market SaaS company, stop optimizing self-serve activation in isolation. Start asking which PLG signups are worth handing to sales, and build the product signals that make that handoff obvious.

The companies winning in 2025 aren't picking PLG or sales-led. They're picking both, in the right sequence.

What's your team's current split between self-serve and sales-assisted revenue?

#GTM #SaaS #ProductStrategy
